source: Derived from error patterns across CB SAT practice (data/cb-corpus/)

mistakeCategories.name: Took the percent of the wrong base
mistakeCategories.description: Student applies the percent to the new amount instead of the original (or vice-versa).
mistakeCategories.studentExample: 20% increase then 20% decrease assumed to return to start.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Compute the percent change using the wrong base (final vs original).

mistakeCategories.name: Confused percentage points with percent change
mistakeCategories.description: Student treats a change from 20% to 25% as a 5% change instead of 25%.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Report the difference in percentage points as if it were the percent change.

mistakeCategories.name: Inverted the ratio
mistakeCategories.description: Student sets up the proportion with the parts flipped.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Produce the answer that results from inverting the ratio.

mistakeCategories.name: Computed the part, not the requested total
mistakeCategories.description: Student finds the increase/discount amount but the question asks for the final value.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Finds the $15 discount but the question wants the $85 final price.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Give the change amount when the question asks for the resulting total (or vice-versa).
